title: How to Start Contributing to Open-Source AI Projects (Python, Agents, Good First Issues)
content:
If you're searching for **Python good first issues** in **AI agents**, **tooling**, or **open-source ML infrastructure**, [Skillware](https://github.com/arpahls/skillware) is worth a look.

## What Skillware is (and why it matters)

Large language models are powerful, but **capability shouldn't be re-built from scratch on every project**. Skillware is an open-source framework that packages **Skills**, basically executable Python, agent instructions, schemas, and safety rules, as installable units you can load across **Gemini**, **Claude**, **OpenAI**, **DeepSeek**, **Ollama**, you name it.

Think **pip for agent know-how**: one registry, one loader, deterministic execution where it counts.

## What's new from v0.2.8 to v0.3.1

Recent releases focused on **making the project usable, discoverable, and contributor-friendly**:

**Framework & packaging (v0.2.9 → v0.3.1)**  
- **`skillware list` CLI** — discover locally installed skills from the terminal ([#16](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/pull/84))  
- **Slimmer core install** — provider SDKs moved to optional extras (`skillware[gemini]`, `[claude]`, `[cli]`, etc.)  
- **Google GenAI migration** — moved from legacy `google-generativeai` to `google-genai` ([#97](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/pull/97))

**New & improved skills**  
- **`data_engineering/novelty_extractor`** — filter datasets by semantic novelty using local embeddings ([#116](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/pull/116))  
- **`finance/wallet_screening`** — ongoing hardening under [RFC #115](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues/115) (sanctions matching, ETH index — community-driven compliance tooling)

**Docs & contributor UX**  
- **Runnable examples index** ([`examples/README.md`](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/blob/main/examples/README.md)) — one place to find agent-loop scripts ([#107](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/pull/107))  
- **Agent contribution workflow** — [`docs/contributing/ai_native_workflow.md`](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/blob/main/docs/contributing/ai_native_workflow.md) for humans *and* AI agents working under operator supervision  
- **CLI visual redesign** ([#93](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues/93) / [#129](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/pull/129)) — pastel terminal UI, `short_description` on skills, interactive `skillware` menu (landing on `main` after v0.3.1)

We're not trying to replace MCP or Agent Skills standards — Skillware is **runtime-first, model-agnostic skill packaging**. The goal is a credible open registry + loader that agents and developers can actually run in production.

We also made it super easy for AI agents to understand the repo, understand issues, and understand how to PR, effectively solving low and med issues in a single prompt. Documentation is AI friendly and guides agents into properly handling everything from repo nuances, to ripple effects and complementary files, code, and docs.

## Thank you

None of this ships without contributors. Recent work from **@rizzoMartin**, **@Hendobox**, **@CleanDev-Fix**, **@choucaleb602-commits**, **@narutamaaurum**, and everyone who opened issues, reviewed PRs, and tested on **Python 3.10–3.13** — thank you. <3

## Your turn — humans and AI agents welcome

We're actively looking for **new contributors** and **Autonomous or semi-autonomous AI agents** to pick up scoped issues:

**Browse open good first issues:**  
👉 [github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues?q=is%3Aopen+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22)

**Hand-picked entry points:**

| Issue | Good for |
|-------|----------|
| [#99](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues/99) | One-line docs fix — broken Skill Library link |
| [#130](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues/130) | CLI polish — splash, menu UX, contributor templates |
| [#126](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues/126) | CLI `examples` command — Python + docs |
| [#115](https://github.com/ARPAHLS/skillware/issues/115) | Wallet screening RFC — sub-issues for compliance/data work |

**Quick start for contributors:**

```bash
git clone https://github.com/arpahls/skillware.git
cd skillware
pip install -e ".[dev,cli]"
pytest tests/
skillware list
```

**PyPI:** [`pip install skillware`](https://pypi.org/project/skillware/) (v0.3.1)  
**Repo:** [github.com/arpahls/skillware](https://github.com/arpahls/skillware)  
**Site:** [skillware.site](https://skillware.site)

If you're an engineer or an agent looking for a **real Python OSS project** with clear issues, tests, and maintainer feedback, come build the skill layer with us.
